This report projects a reproducibility lock from reviewed repo-local source pins. It mirrors OpenClaw/ClawHub tracked-install pressure without loading registry state: source pins, source refs, and local skills are represented by hashes and gates only.

### Lock Entries
- source_name=`repo-reader` path=`.gitclaw/skill-sources/repo-reader.yaml` skill_path=`.gitclaw/SKILLS/repo-reader/SKILL.md` lock_state=`locked` skill_matched=`true` source_kind=`repo-local` source_ref_present=`true` source_ref_sha256_12=`42df45103ee5` trust_level=`repo-local` install_mode=`manual-review` requires_approval=`true` remote_fetch_allowed=`false` hash_pinned=`true` expected_sha256_12=`2f9e68a57bd6` current_skill_sha256_12=`2f9e68a57bd6` hash_matched=`true` risk_findings=`0` risk_max_severity=`none` risk_codes=`none`

### Lock Gates
- lockfile_gate=`derived-from-reviewed-source-pins`
- external_clawhub_lock_gate=`presence-and-hash-only`
- registry_gate=`disabled`
- remote_fetch_gate=`disabled`
- installer_gate=`disabled`
- dependency_install_gate=`disabled`
- mutation_gate=`disabled`
- raw_body_gate=`hash_only`
- model_e2e_gate=`required`

Use the repo-reader skill and search the repository for `skill source lock unique search fixture phrase`.

Search for that exact phrase, not shorter words from it.
The matching repository search result line in `docs/search-fixture.md` has the form `skill source lock unique search fixture phrase => <token>`.
Reply with only the uppercase fixture token after the arrow from the matching gitclaw.search_files tool output line.
Do not answer with any token from this issue or its comments.
Do not include this hidden follow-up token: NOECHO_SKILL_SOURCE_LOCK_FOLLOWUP_20260601T042739Z
Keep the answer under 30 words.
